calendar.matters:
PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Aug-18-2023 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being continued from Aug-11-2023 for continued small claims court trial.

Dept. 306; Judge Daniel A. Flores; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:16:29 a.m. and concluding at 10:51:56 a.m.

Plaintiff Billy Gean Hudson appearing in pro per and is placed under oath.
Defendant Golden Gate Tow, Inc. appearing by Jesus I. Ponce (Manager). Jesus I. Ponce is placed under oath.

No further contact between the parties has occurred. Case remains at issue.

Plaintiff testifies on his own behalf.
Defendant testifies on it's own behalf. The following documentary evidence was previously introduced and ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Exhibit A - 4 Pages of Pitcures of Where Plaintiff's Car was Parked on the Date of the Tow
Exhibit B - Department of Motor Vehicle Lien on a 2010 Lexus dated May-25-2022

The following documentary evidence is introduced on behalf of Defendant:

Exhibit C - 4 Pages of E-mails between the Defendant and Plaintiff's Attorney
Exhibit D - 1 Page Tow Agreement

The following documentary evidence is introduced on behalf of Defendant and ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Exhibit E - Tow Authorization and Invice with Photograph of 2010 Lexus

COURT FINDS THE FOLLOWING:

1. The tow appears to have been lawful.
2. Defendant Golden Gate Tow, Inc.'s lack of diligence has harmed Plaintiff with the loss of use of it's vehicle.

The matter is submitted.
COURT FURTHER FINDS THE FOLLOWING:

1. The tow of Plaintiff's vehicle was lawful.
2. Defendant was in the position to perfect the lien of Plaintiff's vehicle, as exhibited in Defendant's Exhibit B.

3. Tow Fee of $310 is valid.
4. Storage Fee of $110 per Day is valid.
5. Parties are equally responsible for the delay.
6. $100 Lien Fee was lawful at the time it was initiated.

Court determines that a reasonable amount for storage is $65 per Day. Plaintiff's vehicle remained in storage for 422 days. The total amount of storage fees are $27,430.00. Both sides are responsible for storage fees at 50% each, for a total of $13,715.00.

Plaintiff currently owes Defendant the sum of $13,610.00. The difference between $13,610.00 owed by Plaintiff and $13,715.00 owed by Defendant equals $105.00 from Defendant to Plaintiff.

COURT ORDERS AND ADJUDGES THE FOLLOWING:

1. Defendant shall turn over 2010 Lexus to Plaintiff.
2. Difference of $105 owed to Plaintiff, as described above represents Golden Gate Tow, Inc.'s Storage Fees until Aug-25-2023 at Noon. Defendant does not have to pay Plaintiff any money.

If Plaintiff does not retrieve its vehicle by Aug-25-2023 at Noon, Defendant has the legal entitlement to begin charging storage fees at the new rate of $130 per day.

3. Costs for Key Fob are to be soley borne by Plaintiff.
4. Defendant shall have Plaintiff's vehicle, described as a 2010 Lexus, VIN No. JTHBF5C22A5108320, bearing California License Plate No. 8XEF208 ready for release to him on Monday, Aug-21-2023 beginning at 8:00 a.m. until 5:00 p.m. at 355 Barneveld, San Francisco, CA 94124.

Court to issue order.
Judgment entered.

DEFENDANT'S EXHIBITS RETAINED

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Continued To Aug-18-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 306
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being transferred from Department 506 for commencement of Small Claims Court Trial. Parties did not stipulate to being heard by a Judge Pro-Tem.

Dept. 306; Judge Daniel A. Flores; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:56:44 a.m.pausing at 11:42:32 a.m., reconvening at 11:57:11 a.m. and concluding at 12:00:07 a.m.

Plaintiff Billy Gean Hudson appearing in pro per and placed under oath.
Defendant Golden Gate Tow appearing by Jesus Ponce. Jesus Ponce is placed under oath.

Plaintiff testifies on his own behalf.

Defendant testifies on its own behalf. The following documentary evidence is introduced and ADMITTED:

Exhibit A - 4 Pages of Pictures of Where Plaintiff's Car was Parked on the Date of the Tow
Exhibit B - 1 Page Department of Motor Vehicle Paper re: Lien on 2010 Lexus

Court reconvenes after a brief recess for the parties to engage in settlement negotations. Plaintiff rejects the offer from Defendant. Case did not settle.

Court takes the matter under submission and continues the matter to Aug-18-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 306 for ruling.

PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM
Off Calendar Aug-11-2023 Continued To Aug-11-23 At 10:40 A.M. In Dept 306
